testcase.name: createEnvParser > exposes the parsed env object
createEnvParser > produces nested objects from double-underscore keys
createEnvParser > get() resolves a flat key using its original casing
createEnvParser > get() resolves a nested key via dot syntax
createEnvParser > get() resolves a nested key via double underscore
createEnvParser > dot syntax and double underscore resolve to the same value
createEnvParser > get() returns an object when the path resolves to a namespace
createEnvParser > get() returns undefined for a missing flat key
createEnvParser > get() returns undefined for a missing nested path
createEnvParser > get() supports ?? fallback for renamed keys
createEnvParser > get() prefers the primary key when both are present
createEnvParser > map() builds a config shape from resolved get() calls
createEnvParser > map() supports ?? fallback for renamed keys
createEnvParser > map() supports default values for optional keys
createEnvParser > deprecate() warns locally when the old key is still present
testcase.system-out: [@theholocron/utils-env] (debugLog) - test-app loaded 1 key(s) from environment { keys: [ [32m'new_db_url'[39m ] }

testcase.name: createEnvParser > deprecate() is silent when the old key is absent
createEnvParser > deprecate() is silent in deployed environments
debug > debugLog > logs key count and names when local and DEBUG=true
debug > debugLog > never logs values — only key names
debug > debugLog > flattens nested keys to dot-path notation
debug > debugLog > is silent when DEBUG is not set
debug > debugLog > is silent when DEBUG=false
debug > debugLog > is silent when DEBUG=0
debug > debugLog > is silent in a deployed environment even when DEBUG=true
debug > debugLog > accepts "1" as a truthy DEBUG value
debug > debugLog > accepts "yes" as a truthy DEBUG value
debug > debugLog > accepts any casing of DEBUG value
debug > debugLog > handles an empty env object gracefully
debug > warnMissingKey > warns with the key name when local and DEBUG=true
debug > warnMissingKey > is silent when DEBUG is not set
debug > warnMissingKey > is silent in a deployed environment even when DEBUG=true
debug > warnMissingKey > includes the missing key name in the warning message
debug > debugDeprecation > warns when the old key is present in the env
debug > debugDeprecation > is silent when the old key is not present in the env
debug > debugDeprecation > is silent in a deployed environment
debug > debugDeprecation > does not require DEBUG to be set — deprecation warnings always fire locally
debug > debugDeprecation > only checks top-level keys — nested dot-path keys are not matched
debug > debugDeprecation > includes both old and new key names in the warning
environment > normalize > normalizes canonical names
environment > normalize > normalizes aliases
environment > normalize > returns undefined for unknown strings
environment > get > returns DEFAULT_ENVIRONMENT for unknown value
environment > get > uses ENVIRONMENT when set
environment > get > normalizes aliases from ENVIRONMENT
environment > get > falls back to ENV when ENVIRONMENT is missing
environment > get > falls back to NODE_ENV when ENVIRONMENT and ENV are missing
environment > get > falls back to DEFAULT_ENVIRONMENT when no env vars are set
environment > get > uses ENVIRONMENT first when multiple env vars are set
environment > isDeployed > returns qa for "true"
environment > isDeployed > returns test for "true"
environment > isDeployed > returns staging for "true"
environment > isDeployed > returns prod for "true"
environment > isDeployed > returns production for "true"
environment > isDeployed > returns local for "false"
environment > isDeployed > returns dev for "false"
environment > isDeployed > returns development for "false"
environment > isDeployed > uses environment variables when no input is provided
environment > isDeployed > falls back to default environment when env is invalid
DotenvLoader > implements the EnvLoader interface — load() returns a record
DotenvLoader > returns process.env after loading
DotenvLoader > reflects all stubbed env vars in the returned record
DotenvLoader > returns undefined for keys not present in process.env
DotenvLoader > calls dotenv/config as a side effect
DotenvLoader > is safe to call load() multiple times — dotenv is a no-op on repeat calls
DotenvLoader > satisfies the EnvLoader interface structurally
buildEnvObject > builds a flat object from simple keys
buildEnvObject > builds a nested object from double-underscore keys
buildEnvObject > coerces values when parseValues is true
buildEnvObject > skips undefined values
buildEnvObject > throws on scalar-to-namespace collision
buildEnvObject > merges multiple keys into the same nested namespace
coerceValue > coerces "true" to boolean true
coerceValue > coerces "false" to boolean false
coerceValue > coerces numeric strings to numbers
coerceValue > leaves regular strings as-is
collectKeys > returns empty array for empty object
collectKeys > returns flat keys for a flat object
collectKeys > returns dot-path keys for a nested object
collectKeys > handles multiple levels of nesting
collectKeys > handles mixed flat and nested keys
collectKeys > handles multiple nested branches
collectKeys > respects a custom prefix
getByPath > returns a primitive at a leaf path
getByPath > returns a nested object at an intermediate path
getByPath > returns undefined when a path segment doesn't exist
getByPath > returns undefined when traversing through a primitive
getByPath > returns undefined for a completely missing top-level key
isEnvObject > returns true for an empty object
isEnvObject > returns true for a flat EnvObject
isEnvObject > returns true for a nested EnvObject
isEnvObject > returns false for a string
isEnvObject > returns false for an empty string
isEnvObject > returns false for a number
isEnvObject > returns false for zero
isEnvObject > returns false for a boolean true
isEnvObject > returns false for a boolean false
normalizeKey > splits dot syntax into a traversal path
normalizeKey > splits double underscores into a traversal path
normalizeKey > uppercased double underscores normalize the same as lowercase
normalizeKey > single underscores produce a flat key — never a traversal path
normalizeKey > uppercased flat key lowercases correctly
normalizeKey > dot syntax and double underscores produce the same path
parseKey > preserves single underscores as part of the key name
parseKey > splits on double underscores into a nested path
parseKey > handles mixed single and double underscores
parseKey > lowercases all segments
parseKey > handles a single segment with no underscores
setDeep > writes a string value at a single-segment path
setDeep > writes a number value at a single-segment path
setDeep > writes a boolean value at a single-segment path
setDeep > overwrites an existing primitive at a single-segment path
setDeep > creates intermediate namespaces for a nested path
setDeep > writes a value at a three-segment path
setDeep > merges into an existing namespace without overwriting siblings
setDeep > writes multiple values into the same deeply nested namespace
setDeep > does not mutate other top-level keys
setDeep > throws when an intermediate key already holds a scalar value
setDeep > includes the colliding key path in the scalar-to-namespace error message
setDeep > throws at the first colliding segment, not the full path
setDeep > throws when the leaf key already holds a namespace object
setDeep > includes the full path in the namespace-to-scalar error message
setDeep > mutates the target in place rather than returning a new object
